channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: SRT vs MOQT: Low-Latency Video Transport Comparison
item.description: Questions about SRT vs MOQT come up often when engineers evaluate low latency video transport options. As a Lead Real-Time Video Architect working on MOQ development at Red5, I ran into this...

item.title: You Might Not Need TanStack Query
item.description: When I teach web development, I always spend time on one of the most elegant moves in the history of software architecture: the shift from Web 1.0 application servers to REST. Early application...

item.title: Sonos is releasing its first consumer speakers since 2024
item.description: Sonos is returning to focus on the system that made it a driving force in whole-home audio. And to support that system, it's releasing its first new consumer speakers since 2024, the Sonos Play...

item.title: Understanding the Event Loop, Synchronous vs Asynchronous Code (Explained for Beginners)
item.description: JavaScript Interview Puzzle #3 Over the past few days in this JavaScript Interview Puzzle series, we explored some small pieces of code that behave in surprising ways. Each puzzle looked simple....

item.title: Squeezing Microseconds: A Practical Guide to Java/.NET Performance Tuning
item.description: If you're calling Java from .NET (or vice versa), you've probably noticed that cross-runtime calls aren't free. The bridge overhead itself is tiny — microseconds per call — but when you're making...

item.title: iRobot’s Roomba Mini can clean where its larger robovacs can’t reach
item.description: iRobot has announced its first new robot since the company filed for bankruptcy last December and was later acquired by China's Picea Robotics. At just 9.5-inches in diameter, the new Roomba Mini...

item.title: I Gave My Trading Agent Memory and It Made Everything Worse
item.description: How similarity-based recall amplifies LLM confirmation bias, and a simple mechanism that breaks the feedback loop. I spent two days and $73 watching an LLM trading agent destroy itself with its...

item.title: New "LeakyLooker" Flaws in Google Looker Studio Could Enable Cross-Tenant SQL Queries
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed nine cross-tenant vulnerabilities in Google Looker Studio that could have permitted attackers to run arbitrary SQL queries on victims' databases and...

item.title: Training a Neural Network in 16-bit Fixed Point on a 1982 BBC Micro
item.description: The source code for this post can be found on GitHub. In my previous post on neural networks we went through the basic concepts and simple mathematics involved in training and running a neural...

item.title: Make your server-rendered website feel like an SPA — with 5KB of JavaScript
item.description: Make your server-rendered website feel like an SPA — with 5KB of JavaScript You've built a server-rendered website. PHP, Python, Ruby, Go — doesn't matter. It works. It's fast. It's SEO-friendly....

item.title: DumbQuestion.ai - Self-Awareness, Prompt Injection, Search Intent... and darkness
item.description: Continued from Part 2 (and Part 1) ... Building DumbQuestion.ai wasn't just about choosing the right LLM and calibrating personas. Once those were working, I hit a series of fun technical problems...

item.title: Adobe is debuting an AI assistant for Photoshop
item.description: Adobe is also adding new AI-powered image editing features to Firefly.

item.title: X's API: From the Platform That Built Modern Social Development to the One That Burned It Down
item.description: The rise, fall, and cautionary lessons of the most influential API in social media history. There was a time when "Twitter API" was synonymous with innovation. In 2007, Twitter opened one of the...

item.title: Gemini 2.5 Flash vs Claude 3.7 Sonnet: 4 Production Constraints That Made the Decision for Me
item.description: An evaluation of the Gemini 2.5 flash and Claude 3.7 Sonnet model for an agentic engine. I had a simple rule when choosing an LLM for Ozigi: don't pick based on benchmark leaderboards. After my v2...

item.title: Zoom introduces an AI-powered office suite, says AI avatars for meetings arrive this month
item.description: Zoom is also introducing a real-time deepfake detection tech for meetings

item.title: Google rolls out new Gemini capabilities to Docs, Sheets, Slides, and Drive
item.description: The idea behind the new features is to make the apps more personal and capable to help users get things done faster, right within the platforms themselves.

item.title: Prioritizing energy intelligence for sustainable growth
item.description: Loudoun County, Virginia, once known for its pastoral scenery and proximity to Washington, DC, has earned a more modern reputation in recent years: The area has the highest concentration of data...

item.title: I Used Google’s New Gemini-Powered ‘Help Me Create’ Tool in Docs. It’s Great at Corporate-Speak
item.description: Google has injected Docs, Drive, Sheets, and Slides with its Gemini assistant, adding new features that can pull information from your emails and the web to help you draft content. I gave it a whirl.

item.title: Gemini in Google Sheets just achieved state-of-the-art performance.
item.description: Today we announced new beta features for Gemini in Sheets to help you create, organize and edit entire sheets, from basic tasks to complex data analysis — just describe …

item.title: Google’s Gemini AI is getting a bigger role across Docs, Sheets, and Slides
item.description: Google is embedding its Gemini AI assistant even more deeply within its Workspace apps. The changes, which are rolling out to Google Workspace and AI plan subscribers, include a new Gemini chat...

item.title: MacBook Neo review: the Mac for the masses
item.description: The MacBook Neo is basically the M1 MacBook Air all over again. That laptop changed the game in 2020, and became the default option for just about anyone who wanted a great all-around...

item.title: Project Hail Mary is popcorn sci-fi at its best
item.description: Have you ever wondered what Arrival would be like if it was also a goofy buddy comedy? Me neither. And yet the strange combination somehow fits together in Project Hail Mary. The movie follows a...

item.title: You can now ask Photoshop’s AI assistant to edit images for you
item.description: Adobe announced more agentic AI features for its Creative Cloud apps this week, allowing users to edit images and documents by describing the changes to a chatbot. A native AI-assistant is now...

item.title: Wordle’s creator is back with a game that aims to ease players into cryptic crosswords
item.description: A few years after selling Wordle to the New York Times, creator Josh Wardle has returned with a new word game - albeit one that's quite a bit trickier. Wardle and a team of collaborators have just...

item.title: The Download: AI’s role in the Iran war, and an escalating legal fight
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. How AI is turning the Iran conflict into theater Much of...

item.title: Sandbar secures $23M Series A for its AI note-taking ring
item.description: Sandbar aims to ship the Stream, which can be used to take notes, chat with an AI assistant, and for media playback, this summer.

item.title: How to Stop AI Data Leaks: A Webinar Guide to Auditing Modern Agentic Workflows
item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is no longer just a tool we talk to; it is a tool that does things for us. These are called AI Agents. They can send emails, move data, and even manage software on...

item.title: The Zero-Day Scramble is Avoidable: A Guide to Attack Surface Reduction
item.description: You can't control when the next critical vulnerability drops. You can control how much of your environment is exposed when it does. The problem is that most teams have more internet-facing...

item.title: APT28 Uses BEARDSHELL and COVENANT Malware to Spy on Ukrainian Military
item.description: The Russian state-sponsored hacking group tracked as APT28 has been observed using a pair of implants dubbed BEARDSHELL and COVENANT to facilitate long‑term surveillance of Ukrainian military...

item.title: Meta’s deepfake moderation isn’t good enough, says Oversight Board
item.description: Meta's methods for identifying deepfakes are "not robust or comprehensive enough" to handle how quickly misinformation spreads during armed conflicts like the Iran war. That's according to the...
